rule_of_law.definition: CJ's most-repeated organizing concept: law as supreme over force, applied to specific institutional contexts (AFP loyalty, ICC obligations, international order, anti-authoritarianism).

signature_phrases.phrase: the rule of law
twin beacons of liberty and prosperity
au contraire
maraming salamat po
the bedrock of the rule of law
civilian authority is, at all times, supreme over the military
unreasonable restrictions on freedom favor the enemies of the rule of law
the quickest way to lose the red sands of youth is to squeeze the hand too tightly
freedom and food, justice and jobs, liberty and prosperity
i should know because when i was young, i was extremely poor
might is right
right is might
the rule of force
the new bully in asia
which will prevail?
justice and jobs; freedom and food; ethics and economics; liberty and prosperity
the indispensability and inseparability
the freedom from tyranny and the freedom from poverty
the freedom from fear and the freedom from hunger
under the rule of law in a democracy, not under the rule of force in a monocracy

key_stances.claim: Unlike the restive militaries of Myanmar, Thailand, and elsewhere, the AFP has been faithful to the Charter and the rule of law — defending Cory Aquino from at least eight putsch attempts, helping topple Estrada when People Power 2 had popular support, and stopping revolutionary-government attempts.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: comparative institutional praise + historical enumeration

key_stances.claim: China has replaced Great Britain as the new bully in Asia — by swarming the WPS with 200+ ships and prohibiting Philippine fishing in its own EEZ, China is imposing 'Might is right' (rule of force), the same adage that Britain once forced on China through the Opium Wars and Unequal Treaties.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: historical reversal / poetic irony — China inflicting on others what was once inflicted on it

key_stances.claim: FLP scholarships are unique because recipients are judged not only for academic excellence but also for commitment to promote the underlying philosophy of safeguarding liberty and nurturing prosperity under the rule of law.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: differentiation framing (academic + philosophical commitment as joint criteria)

key_stances.claim: ASEAN's adherence to the rule of law — its abhorrence of armed, forcible, insidious, and bullying tactics, including 'gray tactics' — is the foundation of regional peace; ASEAN believes in covenants, treaties, judicial and arbitral tribunals, NOT in the rule of the mob, propaganda, falsehood, or nuclear weapons.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: negative enumeration (NOT… NOT… NOT…) as rhetorical structure

key_stances.claim: Three judicial systems are simultaneously besieged: the Philippine legal system (ICC challenge), US courts (Trump cases), and the ICJ (Russia-Ukraine and South Africa-Israel genocide cases) — making 2024 a stress test of the rule of law worldwide.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: global-tableau framing organizing disparate cases under one thesis

key_stances.claim: China is constricted despite being a superpower — surrounded by hostile neighbors with only North Korea, Cambodia, and Mongolia as dependable allies; it needs the SCS to control sea lanes for its economy, to defend against US militarism, and to provide submarine passage to the Pacific and Indian oceans.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-geopolitical analysis of Chinese strategic compulsion

key_stances.claim: The Philippine Supreme Court — traditionally a bulwark of conservatism — is on a 'crucial but rocky road to liberalism,' surprising given that 13 of its members are 'insiders' from the judicial ranks expected to be conservative.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-institutional surprise + insider/outsider classification

key_stances.claim: The Supreme Court's 13-0-2 decision declaring the Sara Duterte impeachment unconstitutional is not yet final — the OSG has filed an MR, and the Court should explain its position on three serious issues: alleged factual errors about the one-year bar, the self-serving effect of making it nearly impossible to impeach high officials, and the operative-fact doctrine.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structured three-issue critique + reminder that finality has not attached

anecdotes.text: Peddling newspapers and shining shoes as a poor schoolboy; selling textbooks to law-school classmates to afford his own
Being tempted by Karl Marx as a poor youth before Fr. Nolan and Dr. Roces at FEU taught him the superiority of democracy
Gesmundo asking him to continue as ALA Philippines chair because of the Strategic Plan for Judicial Innovation
Negotiating a compromise — 'an ancient relic' staying until end of 2023, with Gesmundo taking over 2024
Recalling that 'when I was a student, priests faced the altar during masses, mumbling unintelligible (to the faithful) Latin that frankly turned me off' — pre-Vatican II Catholic formation
Vatican II reforms making the Church more humane and ecumenical, implemented in the Philippines by PCP 2
Writing the main dissent in La Bugal-B'laan v. Ramos (January 27, 2004) and then writing the majority resolution reversing the Court (December 1, 2004) — a personal example of MR reversal
Estrada asking him in a one-on-one meeting at Jose Pardo's Ayala Alabang home why none of Estrada's own SC appointees dissented from his ouster
Davide and Panganiban inhibiting from the 13-0 Estrada v. Desierto decision
Centenary Lecture Series Phase I bookended by past CJ Narvasa (opening) and incumbent CJ Davide (closing) — a structural-symbolic continuity

liberty_and_prosperity.definition: CJ's signature jurisprudential thesis: liberty and prosperity must always go together — 'one is useless without the other'. The Foundation for Liberty and Prosperity (FLP) embodies this philosophy.

the prosperity side of its philosophy

these twin beacons must always go together; one is useless without the other
give a man a fish and you feed him for a day. teach him to fish and you feed him for a lifetime
save the fisherman
we must never stop dreaming for fear that reality may shatter our dreams
the twin anchors of my being as a lawyer and as a jurist
twin beacons must always go together; one is useless without the other

key_stances.claim: Truth is eternal and limitless — not bound by sovereignty, territory, ideology, or legality; and this universal truth is that humans need both justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, peace and development, liberty and prosperity.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: philosophical universalism + chiasmic-pair enumeration (signature formula)

key_stances.claim: Humans need both political and economic freedoms together — justice and jobs, freedom and food, ethics and economics, liberty and prosperity — one is useless without the other.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical pairing / chiasmic enumeration ('twin beacons') as core philosophical formula

key_stances.claim: FLP scholarships are unique because recipients are judged not only for academic excellence but also for commitment to promote the underlying philosophy of safeguarding liberty and nurturing prosperity under the rule of law.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: differentiation framing (academic + philosophical commitment as joint criteria)

key_stances.claim: He owes Marixi Prieto his opinion-writing career — 15 years of weekly Sunday columns without fail, despite occasional illnesses, five travel trips abroad yearly, and work as officer/director/adviser in over 25 companies and foundations.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: gratitude statement + consistency-as-credential

key_stances.claim: FLP is expanding its reach this school year to the 'prosperity side' of its philosophy via the Esmel Fellowships — bringing the foundation's twin-beacons formula into operational balance between liberty (legal scholarships) and prosperity (graduate management/economics fellowships).
key_stances.rhetorical_move: operational-balance framing of philanthropic expansion

key_stances.claim: Lawyers and the public should not fear AI disruption — humans will not be replaced by AI per se, but can be replaced by a person using AI; the key is liberty of thought combined with AI's algorithmic power.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: endorsement of MVP's framing + complement-not-replace doctrine

key_stances.claim: In law and the magistracy, a combination of general education and specialized knowledge is essential — 'in no other profession is this more critical'; lawyers and magistrates must have well-rounded knowledge of history, literature, philosophy, humanities, social sciences, ethics, mathematics, IT, economics, medicine, biogenics, physics and natural sciences.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: Hand-citation framing + enumerative-disciplines requirement

key_stances.claim: The Supreme Court has a twofold responsibility: first to review lower court decisions and correct reversible errors; second, to pass upon acts of any branch or instrumentality of government to determine grave abuse of discretion 'amounting to lack or excess of jurisdiction.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-doctrinal restatement of Article VIII Section 1

persuading 14 colleagues from initial 3-vote support to unanimous concurrence in Tañada v. Angara

personal_formation.definition: Autobiographical formation arc: Sampaloc poverty, 15-centavo bus fare, UST/FEU scholarships, 1956 FEU strike encounter with Salonga, 1960 bar exam wading, Yale-denied, law practice, 1992-1995 JBC failures, 1995 Ramos appointment.

key_stances.claim: Education, hard work, and God transformed him from a poor newsboy to the highest judicial position in the country — a metamorphosis from shoeshine boy to corporate boardrooms, from religious ignoramus to Pontifical Council member, from frustrated graduate-school applicant to father of five US-pedigreed graduates.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structured biographical transformation list (from X to Y, from X to Y...)

key_stances.claim: The opening message of the Ageless Passion musicale captured his life mantra: 'Good news, bad news, who knows? I will just keep on doing my best and let God do the rest.' — adapted from a folk parable of a farmer and his son experiencing alternating fortunes.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: transmission of personal credo via parable + intergenerational transmission to grandson

key_stances.claim: Salonga was CJ's guru, surrogate father, and lifelong role model — teaching by example rather than words the values of integrity, prudence, and fairness.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: personal eulogy + narrative biography

key_stances.claim: Writing a weekly Inquirer column for ten years without fail — through illness, carpal tunnel, business commitments, speaking engagements, and travel abroad — has been one of the ringing joys of his life and an act of intellectual continuity post-retirement.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: anniversary reflection + consistency claim as discipline-credential

key_stances.claim: The doctrine of constructive resignation should be credited to Justice (later Chief Justice) Reynato Puno who wrote the path-breaking unanimous Estrada-Arroyo decision — Davide and CJ took no part in the 13-0 ruling; any attribution to CJ, whether mockingly or admiringly, 'is simply fake news.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-attribution correction + use of 'fake news' as authoritative dismissal

key_stances.claim: Leni was 'always so soft-spoken, big-hearted, selfless, and so generous,' raising the five children 'with quiet authority' — patient, kind, wise — never yelling, reasoning rather than sermonizing (she said 'eight was the age of reason').
key_stances.rhetorical_move: transmission via daughter's eulogy + structured-virtue enumeration

key_stances.claim: Salonga's most significant teaching is 'not really about the law, as it is about life' — while necessities money can buy (food, shelter, recreation, car, house) are desirable, it is 'far more important to aspire for the things that money cannot buy, like excellence, integrity, honor, dignity and a reverential regard and love for God as the Source of all that is true, good and beautiful.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structured-paired-comparison (necessities vs intangibles) + theistic-source framing of virtue

key_stances.claim: The rendition of justice is 'not only a human necessity; it is a divine essentiality' — when we give people their due, impose penalties, uphold liberty, enforce obligations 'fairly and speedily, without fear or favor — we really do the work of the Lord.' To dispense *quality* justice is to do God's work.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: title-thesis framing + structured-judicial-functions enumeration

key_stances.rhetorical_move: differentiation framing (merit-based, not need-based)

key_stances.claim: The peering questions of the board of judges, the midnight toil of the screening panel, the scholarship prizes from donors, and bits of learning on liberty and prosperity were 'little things' that meant a lot in inspiring the scholars' stories of faith in passing the bar.

key_stances.claim: Communism was appealing in theory but appalling in practice — to a dirt-poor student the Marxist slogan seemed to fulfill his longings, but the masses under communist regimes were maltreated and impoverished.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: personal-experience refutation of ideological appeal

key_stances.claim: FLP is expanding its reach this school year to the 'prosperity side' of its philosophy via the Esmel Fellowships — bringing the foundation's twin-beacons formula into operational balance between liberty (legal scholarships) and prosperity (graduate management/economics fellowships).
key_stances.rhetorical_move: operational-balance framing of philanthropic expansion

key_stances.claim: Fidel V. Ramos was 'a gentleman-patriot' who fully respected his independence and probity — after offering him DOJ Secretary then a SC seat in June 1992, Ramos 'never spoke or influenced me, in any manner, in the discharge of my duties' even when CJ voted against Ramos's political wishes.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: obituary-like tribute + presidential-restraint anecdote (recurring set piece)

key_stances.claim: Life is a strange interplay of light and shadows — when a door closes, a much larger and better window opens; one must keep trying, never give up.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: aphoristic life-philosophy as opening frame, illustrated by personal biography

key_stances.claim: First Lady Louise Araneta-Marcos is 'a real charmer' — she possessed 'vaunted competence as a practicing lawyer and law professor,' dissolved her entrenched law firm before her husband assumed office, and acted spontaneously friendly when CJ formally met her in 2023.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structured-credential admiration + dissolution-of-law-firm emulation (echoing Salonga 1986 / CJ 1995)

Being invited after retirement by Philippine tycoons (most of whom he had not previously met) to join their boards, leading to discovery of their philanthropy and corporate-social-responsibility yearnings

Ramos's June 17, 1992 offer of DOJ Secretary which CJ declined on the grounds he did not campaign or vote for Ramos, leading to Ramos's subsequent SC appointment — a recurring set piece in CJ's autobiographical column corpus
couldn't enter UP because family couldn't afford the 15-centavo Diliman bus fare

First Lady Louise Araneta-Marcos dissolving her entrenched law firm before her husband assumed office — a parallel to Salonga's 1986 disbandment upon PCGG appointment and CJ's own 1995 dissolution upon SC appointment
Meeting the First Lady formally on June 28, 2023 at a Michelangelo Sistine Chapel reproduction exhibit, then again on August 22, 2023 at the Toyota Philippines 35th anniversary

Working with Dr. Jikeong Kang, Prof. Olivier Roche, and Prof. Felipe Calderon at AIM for eight months to develop the Master's in International Business Law curriculum — all three AIM Esmel fellows this year are taking MIBL

judicial_reform.definition: Reforming the bench through structured mnemonics: Four Ins (integrity, intelligence, independence, industry), 3 E-values, 4 Cs of decision-writing, plus institutional reforms (APJR, Benchbook, zero-backlog).

key_stances.claim: Act 136 of 1901 (the Judiciary Law) established for the first time an *independent* judicial hierarchy with the Supreme Court at its apex — prior equivalents (Royal Audiencia, American military regime SC) were 'hardly more than extensions of the executive department.' The Court therefore reckons June 11, 1901 as its founding.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-historical framing of judicial independence

key_stances.claim: Law is territorial in scope but its basic principles — especially on human rights and civil liberties — are universal; the foreign lectures by Dorit Beinisch (Israel) and Pal Solt (Hungary) embody this verity.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: territorial/universal doctrinal pairing + foreign-jurist authority

key_stances.claim: The Supreme Court has a twofold responsibility: first to review lower court decisions and correct reversible errors; second, to pass upon acts of any branch or instrumentality of government to determine grave abuse of discretion 'amounting to lack or excess of jurisdiction.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-doctrinal restatement of Article VIII Section 1

key_stances.claim: The SC reckons its founding on June 11, 1901 because Act 136 was approved by a civil government and established 'an independent judicial hierarchy with the Supreme Court as the apex' — General Order 20 of 1899 under Gen. Elwell Otis was 'really nothing more than an extension of the military authority of the United States Army.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-historical framing of judicial independence as founding-date criterion

key_stances.claim: The centenary celebration on Valentine's Day for SC, CA, and DOJ employees deliberately emphasizes 'na ang mga pinakamalapit sa puso ng Supreme Court ay ang mga kawaning naglilingkod sa hustisya' — those closest to the heart of the SC are the employees serving justice.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: Valentine-themed institutional-affection framing (Tagalog opening)

key_stances.claim: True leadership is sourced from example and inspiration, not from force and domination — 'When we are inspired by some great purpose, by some extraordinary example, our consciousness expands in every direction and discovers greatness in ourselves, which we never dreamed we could transcend.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: example-over-force leadership framing + Longfellow citation

key_stances.claim: The Judicial Reform Program aims to enhance 'the four Ins of a great magistrate: integrity, intelligence, independence and industry' — the first explicit formulation of this mnemonic that becomes CJ's signature framework.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: four-Ins mnemonic introduction (the framework's earliest documented use)

key_stances.claim: The legal profession may be likened to a religious vocation — both demand 'patience, perseverance and prayer' through education, training, sacrifice, and soul-searching to become 'worthy leaders of their faiths.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: religious-vocation parallel framing + structured parallelism (law-school = priestly formation)

key_stances.claim: Duterte's SONA assertion that 'the WPS is ours, no ifs and buts' is a legal and rhetorical victory — he has not waived the Arbitral Award or maritime rights, only deferred aggressive enforcement.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical reframing of presidential ambivalence as preserved option

key_stances.claim: The 2016 Arbitral Award has three operative parts: (1) rejection of the nine-dash line and affirmation of freedom of navigation; (2) recognition of Philippine maritime entitlements (TS, CZ, EEZ, ECS); (3) holding that no disputed feature qualifies as an 'island' so China has no maritime rights even where it has built structures.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal summarization into a triadic structure

key_stances.claim: China will not budge on the Arbitral Award because acceptance would force it to (1) allow freedom of navigation, (2) dismantle SCS installations, and (3) abandon 'historic rights' taught as essential heritage.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-political analysis of Chinese constraints

key_stances.claim: To win negotiation, one must know the other side's arguments, stance, strengths, and weaknesses — Duterte must approach Xi armed with this strategic knowledge of China's legal positions.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: negotiation principle stated as truism + strategic framing

key_stances.claim: Poor and weak states can vie with wealthy and powerful countries only through the might of being right — a steadfast invocation of the rule of law.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: aphoristic restatement of his rule-of-law-as-leverage thesis + appeal to his own teaching authority

key_stances.claim: The Supreme Court's voiding of the JMSU is a subtle reminder to negotiators to comply rigorously with two sacred constitutional safeguards: state full control and supervision over natural resources, and 60% Filipino ownership of qualifying corporations.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal restatement + reframing of decision as governance reminder

key_stances.claim: China is constricted despite being a superpower — surrounded by hostile neighbors with only North Korea, Cambodia, and Mongolia as dependable allies; it needs the SCS to control sea lanes for its economy, to defend against US militarism, and to provide submarine passage to the Pacific and Indian oceans.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-geopolitical analysis of Chinese strategic compulsion

key_stances.claim: The unsaid short and simple answer to why China and the US behave inconsistently with their formal legal positions on UNCLOS and the Arbitral Award is 'national interest' — all states act to protect and project what benefits them, and national interest is not constant; it shifts with present and future aspirations.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: rhetorical-question-and-answer structure + Palmerston-style realism

anecdotes.text: Justice Secretary Menardo Guevarra texting him for clarification on whether ICJ filing would reopen the Arbitral Award
Serving as chair of the Philippine National Group in the PCA alongside Pangalangan, Puno, and Vitug, nominating Iwasawa to the ICJ

key_stances.claim: The Philippine SC was founded in 1901 because that was when 'a tribunal which enjoyed judicial independence, as we know it today, was set up' — even though courts existed under the Spanish conquistadores for almost 400 years and under the US military, those lacked the independence-from-other-branches criterion.

key_stances.claim: Arroyo is the de jure President because Estrada had resigned before her noon January 20, 2001 oath-taking — proven by 'totality test' using prior, contemporaneous, and posterior acts; his press release was a 'valedictory, his final act of farewell. key_stances.rhetorical_move: doctrinal-evidentiary totality framing + finality aphorism (Puno ponencia)

key_stances.claim: The en banc's 8-7 takeover of the Firestone Ceramics case was inappropriate — the movants 'had utterly failed to adduce any legal reason for such referral'; the resulting unanimous denial of Motion for Reconsideration (January 30, 2001) merely 'showed the folly of failing to heed the maxim Sauce for the poor goose should be the same sauce for the rich gander.'
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural-critique of unwarranted en banc absorption + aphorism citation

key_stances.claim: Article VIII Section 1 has two parts: ordinary judicial review (settling actual controversies) and the extraordinary certiorari power to determine grave abuse of discretion — by imposing the second as a duty rather than a mere power, the Constitution mandates judges to be activists.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: structural constitutional bifurcation + duty-vs-power distinction

key_stances.claim: In Manila Prince Hotel v. GSIS, the majority subverted the essence of public bidding — the constitutional Filipino-preference clause applies only when 'the scores are tied,' not when 'the ballgame is over and the foreigners clearly posted the highest score.'

key_stances.claim: Due process protects 'all persons' — citizen or alien, natural or juridical — through five legal requirements: impartial court, jurisdiction over subject matter and person, full opportunity to be heard, and judgment only after being heard.
key_stances.rhetorical_move: enumerative doctrinal definition + constitutional textualism
